exits.label: ◂ back to the Hearth and the road

npcs.name: Sennet
npcs.title: Cartographer of the Dead
npcs.hail: *(a wiry soul in a coat sewn entirely from old map-margins sits cross-legged before a chart so large it spills off the table and onto the grass, inking a coastline that does not exist on any living shore, muttering coordinates to themselves, and they do not look up until your cold shadow falls across the parchment — and then they go very, very still)*

npcs.name: Tibb
npcs.title: the apprentice, worried sick
npcs.hail: *(a young apprentice grinds ink and watches their master with the particular dread of someone who has done the arithmetic on how many more 'field trips to the edge' a mortal body has left in it)*

npcs.name: Goodman Vael
npcs.title: a buyer with patient eyes
npcs.hail: *(a soft-spoken man in good boots stands a little too still by the wagon, admiring the maps with the appraising calm of someone who already knows exactly which one he means to walk away with, and how much he is prepared to make that cost)*

variants.text: *(The apprentice abandons the ink-stone the moment they see your face, and there is naked relief in it, and naked fear.)* …You're *cold.* *(quietly, urgent)* No — *good*, that's — I *mean* it as a *good* thing, hold *still* — *(they study you)* — you've the look of a soul that's *been* somewhere it shouldn't come *back* from. *(they glance at their master, then back, dropping their voice)* That's *exactly* the sort that's going to get me into *trouble*, but it's also the *only* sort that might *help*, so. *(a shaky breath)* Listen. Before you talk to *Sennet* — before you get *swept up* in the *coastlines* and the *coordinates* and the *brilliance* of it all — you should know what you'd be *signing on to.* Because Sennet's going to *want* you. The *instant* they work out what you are, they're going to want you *badly.* And I need *one* soul in this *story* to know what *wanting* you is going to *cost* them.

nodes.speaker: Tibb
nodes.text: *(They keep half an eye on Sennet the whole time, the watchfulness of someone guarding a person from themselves.)* Ask me *quick*, before they notice we're conspiring. *(quietly)* I've been Sennet's apprentice three years. I grind the ink, I keep the wagon, I — *(a breath)* — I count the *days.* Somebody has to.

choices.text: "What is Sennet actually mapping, Tibb? Those aren't coastlines I know."

[INSIGHT] "You're not worried they're a fraud. You're worried they're *right* — that the map is real, and that finishing it means going somewhere a living body doesn't return from. How many more 'field trips' does Sennet have left in them, Tibb?"

*(one who's walked the country on that map)* "Those coastlines Sennet's inking — I know them, Tibb. That's the far shore. The causeway. The edge of the Court. I've *been* there. Which means your master isn't mad, and isn't dying of a delusion — they're dying of being *correct*, and reaching for a place only a soul like me can actually *reach.*"
"Tell me about the buyer. Goodman Vael. He's been admiring those maps a long time."
"I'll keep your master's measure in mind, Tibb."

nodes.speaker: Tibb
nodes.text: *(They lower their voice further, and despite the dread there's pride in it.)* …The *afterlife.* *(barely)* Sennet maps the *country of the dead.* *(they gesture at the great spilling chart)* Not *speculation* — not a *priest's* diagram of *heavens* and *hells.* *Survey work.* Sennet goes to the *edges* — the *causeways*, the *grey* shores, the places where the *living* world *thins* into the *other* — and they *stand* there, as close as a *breathing* soul can get *without crossing*, and they *measure.* Sightlines. Bearings. The *shape* of the far bank. *(they shake their head, half in awe, half in terror)* And the *map* is *good*, traveler. That's the *horror* of it. Other cartographers laugh — 'you can't *chart* the *dead*' — but I've watched *marked* souls glance at Sennet's *causeway* map and go *white*, because it's *right*, it's *exactly* right, down to the *last reed.* *(quietly)* Sennet is making the *first* true *atlas* of *where we all go.* And it is *killing* them to do it, one *edge* at a time.

nodes.speaker: Tibb
nodes.text: *(The young face crumples, and the secret they've been carrying alone comes out in a rush.)* …*Two.* *(barely)* Maybe *three.* That's the count. *(they wipe their eyes, furious)* Every *survey* takes a *little.* You can't *stand* at the *edge* of death and *stare* across it to *measure* the far bank without the *cold* coming *back* into you a *little*, every *time* — Sennet calls it 'exposure', laughs it off, says a *surveyor* expects to lose a few *fingers* to *frostbite*, except it's not *fingers*, it's *years*, it's *warmth*, it's — *(their voice breaks)* — the last *survey*, at the *grey* river, Sennet came *back* and didn't *speak* for a *day*, just *stared*, and I *thought* — I *thought* that was *it*, I thought the *edge* had *kept* them. *(they grip your sleeve)* They *won't* stop. The map's *almost* done — just the *Court* left, the *deep* country, the part *no* surveyor has *ever* charted because *no* surveyor has *ever* come *back* from *standing* close enough — and Sennet means to *go.* *(desperate)* And it will *kill* them. *Unless* — *(they look at you, the cold soul, with terrible hope)* — unless they had a *guide* who could *walk* the *deep* country *for* them. Who could *come back* and *report.* *(quietly)* You see why I'm *frightened* of you, traveler. You're the *one* thing that could *finish* Sennet's *life's work* — and the *one* thing that could let them *survive* finishing it. I don't *know* which you'll *be.*

nodes.speaker: Tibb
nodes.text: *(They stare at you, and the colour drains from their face, and then floods back as something almost like joy.)* …You've *been.* *(a whisper)* To the *places on the map.* *(they grab both your sleeves)* Then Sennet's *not* mad. I *knew* it — I *knew* the coastlines were *real*, I've watched too many *marked* souls *recognize* them — but to hear it from a *soul that walked them* — *(they're half-laughing, half-weeping)* — do you understand what you *are*, to a *cartographer of the dead?* You're a *surveyor who came back from the uncharted interior.* You're *every* blank space on the map with the words *'here be the Returned'* written across it, walking up to the *wagon* on your *own two feet.* *(fierce, frightened)* Sennet is going to *love* you, traveler. And I am going to have to *decide* whether to *let* them — because a Sennet with a *living guide* to the *deep* country might *finish* the *atlas* and *live* — or might just be *handed the means* to walk *further* in than even *exposure* could take them, *gladly*, *grinning*, with a *better* map than anyone's *ever* had of *exactly* how to *get lost forever.* *(quietly)* Be *careful* with them. They're the *bravest* fool I've ever *loved*, and they have *no* instinct for *self-preservation* whatsoever. It's the *one* country they've *never* bothered to *chart.*

nodes.speaker: Tibb
nodes.text: *(Their face hardens — the apprentice, for once, sounding older than their years.)* …Goodman Vael. *(flatly)* He's been 'admiring the maps' for a *fortnight.* Buys nothing. *Asks* a great deal. *(they glance at the soft-spoken man)* He wants the *causeway* survey, traveler. The *real* one — not the *pretty* copies Sennet sells to *grieving* families who want to know *where* their dead 'went.' He wants the *working* map. The one with the *bearings* to the *edges*, the *thin* places, the *crossings.* *(quietly)* And do you know what a *map of every place the living world thins into death* is *worth*, to the *wrong* buyer? *(they shiver)* It's a *map of every door a clever, cruel soul could prop open.* The *Choir* would *pay* for it — to *seal* the doors, or to *use* them. Worse things than the Choir would pay *more.* *(they meet your eyes)* Sennet thinks they're making an *atlas* of *comfort* — a map so the *bereaved* know their *dead* aren't *nowhere.* They don't *see* that the *same* map, in *Vael's* hands, is a *burglar's* chart of the *cosmos.* *(grimly)* Sennet won't *sell* it. I'm *sure* of that. *Almost* sure. But Sennet is *broke*, and *dying*, and *kind*, and Vael is *patient*, and *patient* is how the *worst* purchases get *made.*

nodes.speaker: Tibb
nodes.text: *(They busy themselves with the ink-stone, not quite meeting your eye.)* …Sennet's *fine.* *(unconvincingly)* Eccentric. *Driven.* All the *great* ones are. *(they grind ink too hard)* I'm not — I'm just the *apprentice*, I don't *diagnose* my *master*, I *grind* the *ink* and *mind* the *wagon.* *(a flicker)* …Ask Sennet *yourself*, if you've questions about Sennet. *(quietly, the worry leaking through despite them)* Just — when you *do* — *look* at their *hands* while they talk. The *left* one. And tell me it's not — *(they stop)* — never mind. *Grind* the ink. That's *my* job. *Watching* is — that's *also* my job, lately. The one nobody *trained* me for.

nodes.speaker: Tibb
nodes.text: *(They nod, and return to the ink-stone, and watch their master's back with the patience of the truly devoted.)* Aye. *Mind* it. *(quietly)* Sennet's *worth* minding. *Best* soul I know, under all the *coordinates.* *(they grind ink)* …Go *talk* to them. They'll *light* up. They *always* light up for a *new* edge of the *map*, and you — *(a tired, fond, frightened smile)* — you're a *whole* uncharted *continent*, walking. Just — bring them *back* from wherever they *take* you in the *talking.* They *go* places, when they talk about the *map.* Sometimes I'm not *sure* they come all the *way* back.

variants.text: *(He turns from the maps with a small, warm, recognizing smile.)* …Ah. A *friend* of tidy things, unless I miss my guess. *(he inclines his head)* Goodman Tallow's circle speaks of a *helpful* soul who understands that *order* is a *kindness.* *(he gestures at the great chart)* Then perhaps you'll *understand* my interest here. The mapmaker has drawn the *one* document the Choir has wanted for *centuries* and never had: a *survey* of every place the living world *thins* into the dead. *(softly)* Every *door.* I am here to *acquire* it — for *safekeeping*, you understand. Such a map is *far* too dangerous to leave in the hands of a *sentimental* surveyor who *sells* copies to *weeping* widows. *(his patient eyes find you)* I could use a *clear* soul to help me *persuade* the mapmaker to part with the *working* original. The Choir rewards its *friends.* You know this. Shall we *do business?*

*(He does not startle at your cold, only studies you with the unhurried appraisal of a man who has bought a great many difficult things.)* A new caller at the wagon. *(pleasantly)* Goodman Vael. I deal in — *(a considered pause)* — *documents.* *Rare* ones. *(he nods at the great spilling map)* And *that*, friend, is the *rarest* I have ever *hunted.* A true survey of the *country of the dead.* *(he smiles, soft)* The mapmaker thinks they make a *comfort* for the *grieving.* I know what they have *actually* made: the *only* accurate chart of every *thin* place, every *crossing*, every *door* between *here* and *there.* *(quietly)* I mean to *own* it. I have been *patient.* Patience is my *whole* method. *(his eyes flick to you, weighing)* You've the look of someone who *travels* such places *personally.* That makes you either my *finest* potential *partner* — or the *one* obstacle I had not *budgeted* for. Which shall it be?

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vael
nodes.text: *(He folds his soft hands and waits, the stillness of a man who has learned that the patient party sets the price.)* Speak your mind, traveler. I find *plain* dealing saves *everyone* the *tedium* of the *dance.* What is the *map* to *you?*

choices.text: [INSIGHT] "You keep saying 'safekeeping.' But a man who wanted that map *sealed* would've gone to the Choir and had it *burned.* You want it *open.* You're not buying a door-map to *lock* the doors, Vael. You're buying it to *use* them. What does a patient man want on the *other side* of death badly enough to chart the way in?"
"What's your offer, Vael? Lay it out. If I'm to be your partner or your obstacle, I'd know the number first."

[INTIMIDATION] "Here's the deal, Vael: you leave. Today. You don't buy the map, you don't 'persuade' a dying mapmaker, you take your patient eyes and your good boots back down the road. I've walked the country on that chart, and I will not have a *burglar's copy* made of it."

*(let the one who's been through the doors answer the one who'd buy them)* "You want a map of the doors of death, Vael. I am one. I've been through and back. So take it from the only surveyor who's walked your merchandise: there is *nothing* on the far side worth the price of the doors standing open. Whatever you're chasing over there — a fortune, a person, a second life — the doors don't lead to *it.* They lead to the *cold.* Buy the map and you'll only learn that *expensively.*"
"We've nothing to discuss, Vael." *(turn back to the wagon)*

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vael
nodes.text: *(For the first time the soft patience flickers, and what shows underneath is not malice but a grief as old and patient as everything else about him.)* …You see clearly. *(quietly)* Most see a *broker.* *(he looks at the map)* You're right. I do not want the doors *sealed.* I want *one* of them *found.* *(a long pause)* My wife went through, traveler. Three winters past. *Unbought* — we were never *godly* people, we had each *other*, we thought that was *enough* — and so she went where the *unclaimed* go, and there was no *coin* and no *priest* and no *door* anyone would *show* me. *(his voice does not rise; that is the worst of it)* I have *money.* I have *patience.* I have *nothing* else, now, and I would spend *all* of it to *stand*, *once*, at the *thin* place *nearest* to wherever she *is*, and *say* a *thing* I did not *finish* saying. *(he meets your eyes)* The mapmaker has charted that thin place. It is on that map, with a *bearing* and a *distance.* I am not a *villain*, traveler. I am a *widower* with a *map-shaped* hole in him. *(quietly)* …But I will *grant* you this: a *grieving* man with the *coordinates* of *death's* doors is *exactly* how a *door* gets *propped open* for *worse* than grief to *follow* him through. *(he looks away)* I had not — I had not let myself *think* that part. You've made me think it. *Damn* you for it.

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vael
nodes.text: *(He produces, without hurry, a purse that is far too heavy for its size.)* Plainly, then. *(he sets it on the wagon's step)* For the *working* causeway survey — the *original*, with the *true* bearings — I will pay the mapmaker *more* than a *lifetime* of selling *comfort-copies* to *widows* would earn. Enough to *stop.* Enough to *rest.* Enough that a *dying* surveyor need never *stand at another cold edge* and lose another *year* to the *exposure.* *(he watches you)* I am, you'll note, offering to *save the mapmaker's life* by *buying the thing that's killing them.* It is a *generous* reading, but it is not a *false* one. *(softly)* You could *help* me make this purchase, traveler — *talk* the stubborn surveyor into the *sensible* thing — and I would see *you* rewarded, and Sennet *retired*, and *everyone* the *better* for it. *(his patient eyes)* Except, of course, whoever I *become* once I have the *map.* But you'd not have to *watch* that part. *Most* don't.

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vael
nodes.text: *(He does not flinch, but he inclines his head, and gathers his too-heavy purse with the unhurried dignity of a man folding one patient hand to wait for a better.)* …As you wish. *(quietly)* I'll not *fence* with a soul that's been *through the doors* — you've *standing* I can't *out-patience.* *(he steps back)* I'll *go.* Today. *(a thin smile)* But understand, traveler — the map *exists*, now. It *cannot* un-exist. And a *dying* mapmaker leaves an *estate*, and *estates* are *sold*, and I am a *very* patient man, and I have *out-waited* *grief* itself for *three winters.* *(he turns to the road)* I can *certainly* out-wait *you.* Mind the *surveyor's* health, friend. The *sooner* it *fails*, the *sooner* the *map* comes to *market* — and you'll not be at *every* auction. *(he goes, and the patience of him is worse than any threat.)*

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vael
nodes.text: *(He lets your threat pass over him like weather over a stone.)* …Threats. *(mildly)* From a soul who clearly *cares* what becomes of that map — which tells me *exactly* how *much* it's worth, and *exactly* where your *softness* is. *(he folds his hands)* I have been *threatened* by *grief*, traveler, every *morning* for *three years*, and *grief* is a far harder *creditor* than *you.* *(patiently)* I'll not *leave* on a *raised voice.* I'll leave when I *own* the *thing* I came for, or when a *better* argument than *fear* persuades me the *price* is too *high.* *(his eyes weigh you)* You've not made that argument. You've only made *noise.* And *noise*, friend, I can *out-wait.* It's the *one* thing I'm *certain* of. *Everything* else I lost three winters ago.

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vael
nodes.text: *(✦ You don't threaten his body or his trade — you name, softly and exactly, the thing his patience is built to outlast: that the wife he's chasing through the doors would not *want* to be *found* that way, would not want her grieving husband to *prop open death* and *follow her in*, would want him to *grieve* and *live* — and the patient man, who has out-waited everything, cannot out-wait *that.*)* *(The too-heavy purse slips from his fingers.)* …She — *(barely)* — she *wouldn't.* *(he presses a soft hand to his mouth)* You're — *gods*, you're *right*, she would have *hated* this, she would have taken the *map* out of my *hands* and *burned* it and *shouted* at me for a *fool*, she *always* — *(his patient composure does not crack so much as *dissolve*, three winters of it, all at once)* — I have spent *everything* I *am* building a *door* she would have *barred* with her *own body.* *(he sinks onto the wagon-step)* I do not *want* the map. *(a whisper)* I want *her*, and the map is the *closest* I can *get*, and you have just shown me the *closest* is a *betrayal* of the *very* soul I'm — *(he covers his face)* …I'll go. *No* purchase. *No* waiting. I'll go *home*, to the *empty* house, and I'll *grieve* her the way she'd have *wanted*, which is *badly*, and *alive*, and *without a map.* *(he rises, hollow and lighter)* …You are a *cruel* soul, to have *seen* that. And the *only* kind one who's *spoken* to me in *three years.* *Thank* you. I think you've *saved* me from a *door* I'd not have *survived.*

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vael
nodes.text: *(He goes very still — the first true stillness, not the patient kind — and looks at you the way a starving man looks at someone who claims the feast is poisoned.)* …*Nothing* worth the price. *(quietly)* You'd *have* me believe the *doors* lead only to *cold.* *(his patient eyes search yours)* But you came *back*, traveler. From the *cold* you describe. *Wearing* it, yes — but *back.* So either the *cold* is *not* the *whole* of it, or you are the *one* exception, and *exceptions*, friend, are *exactly* what a *desperate* man *stakes* his *everything* on. *(a long pause, and something in him wavers)* …And yet. *(barely)* You did *come back wearing it.* And you say it like a *warning*, not a *boast.* A soul who'd been *somewhere good* would not *speak* of it the way you *speak.* *(he looks at the map, and away)* …You have not *talked* me *out* of *grief*, traveler. Nothing can. But you may have talked me out of *paying* for a *door* with the *one* coin I *truly* have *left* — which is the *part* of me that's still *warm* enough to *grieve* at *all.* *(quietly)* …I'll *think* on it. Which, for a *patient* man, is the *most* dangerous thing I do. *You've* made me *think.* I had *budgeted* for *everything* but *that.*

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vael
nodes.text: *(He smiles, soft and impenetrable, and the patience closes back over whatever you reached for.)* *Safekeeping*, traveler. I said *safekeeping*, and I *meant* it. *(smoothly)* You reach for a *darker* story because a *darker* story is more *interesting* than a *collector* who simply *values* a *rare* document. *(he spreads his hands)* But not *every* patient man is a *villain.* Some of us are merely — *thorough.* *(his eyes give you nothing)* Bring me a *sharper* read, if you have one. Or let us *talk* — plainly, like *adults* — about what the *map* is *worth*, and to *whom.* I find *speculation* about my *motives* far less *productive* than *negotiation* about my *price.* And I am, above all, a *productive* man.

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vael
nodes.text: *(He inclines his head, unbothered, and returns his patient gaze to the great map.)* As you wish. *(mildly)* I'll be *here.* Admiring. *(softly, not looking at you)* That is the *thing* about *patience*, traveler — it does not *require* your *participation.* I will *admire* this map *today*, and *tomorrow*, and the *day* the *surveyor's* hand finally *fails* — *(he studies a coastline that should not exist)* — and on *that* day, *participation* will no longer be *anyone's* to *withhold.* *(a soft smile)* Do give my regards to the *mapmaker.* And mind their *health.* I take *such* an *interest* in it. We have, you might say, *opposite* stakes in *exactly* the same *heartbeat.*

variants.text: *(The cartographer's pen stops mid-stroke. Slowly, they look up — past your face, to the *cold* on you, reading it the way they'd read a coastline — and their ink-stained hands begin, very slightly, to *shake*, and not from the exposure.)* …*No.* *(a whisper)* No, that's — *(they rise, knocking the ink, not noticing)* — that's not *exposure-cold*, that's not *edge-cold*, I *know* every grade of the cold a living soul brings *back* from the *thin* places and that is *none* of them. That is — *(their voice cracks with a joy that frightens you both)* — that is *interior* cold. *Deep*-country cold. The cold of a soul that did not *stand at the edge and measure* — a soul that *went IN.* *(they seize your sleeve in trembling hands)* You've been to the *Court.* Haven't you. The *deep* country. The part of my map that is *blank*, that has been *blank* for *thirty years*, that *no* surveyor has *ever* filled because *no* surveyor *comes back* from *standing close enough.* *(tears stand in their eyes)* And you walked *out* of it. On your *own legs.* …Oh. *Oh*, you *beautiful*, *impossible*, *walking continent.* *Sit* *down.* I have been *waiting* for you for my *whole* *life* and I did not *know* it until *this* *instant.*

*(The pen halts. The cartographer lifts their head and *looks* at you — really looks, the surveyor's eye — and goes still as a struck bell.)* …You've been to an *edge.* *(quietly, certain)* A *deep* one. *(they set the pen down with great care)* I can *read* it on a soul, you know — that's the *whole* of my *craft*, reading where a body's *been* by the *cold* it carries *home.* And *you* — *(they rise, slow, wondering)* — you carry the cold of the *causeway's* very *end.* The *last* torch. The *Wall* itself. *(their eyes are bright)* I have *charted* that place from *half a mile back*, through a *spyglass*, *shaking*, losing a *year* of warmth to the *bearing*-taking — and *you* stood at its *foot.* *(they step closer)* Tell me you did not *imagine* it. Tell me the *Wall* is *real*, and *grey*, and set with *faces*, exactly as I've drawn it from *too far away.* *(barely)* Tell me, and I will *love* you forever, because you'll have *confirmed* the *survey* of a *lifetime* with a *single word.*

*(The cartographer glances up, clocks the godless quiet on you, and a slow, delighted, conspiratorial grin spreads across their ink-smudged face.)* …Oh, *you're* unbought. *(they set the pen aside)* *Marvellous.* My *favourite* sort of customer, and my *favourite* sort of *subject.* *(they tap the great map)* Do you know *why?* Because the *godded* think they *know* where they're *going* — straight up some *priest's* tidy *stair* — and they've *no* curiosity about the *map* at all. *(they lean in)* But the *unbought* — *we* — *(a wink)* — *we* go to the *interesting* country. The *uncharted* part. The *Wall*, the *grey* shores, the *deep* dark the *atlases* leave *blank.* *(grinning, morose, alight)* And *that*, friend, is the *only* country worth *mapping.* The *tidy* heavens map *themselves.* It's the *thrown-away* places that need a *surveyor* brave or *foolish* enough to *chart* them. *(they pat the grass beside the map)* Sit. Let's talk about where you and I are *both* going. I've *drawn* it. You'll *want* to see how *close* I got.

*(The wiry soul looks up from a coastline that exists on no living shore, blinks the coordinates out of their eyes, and focuses on you with the bright, slightly unnerving total attention of someone for whom every new person is a *potential data point.*)* …A *caller.* *(they set the pen behind their ear)* Forgive me — I get *lost* in the *work.* Occupational. *(they gesture grandly at the enormous chart spilling off the table into the grass)* Sennet. *Cartographer.* Of a *speciality* most map-houses won't *print* and most *priests* would have me *flogged* for. *(a crooked grin)* I map *where we go.* The *country of the dead* — *surveyed*, not *guessed*, with *real* bearings, taken from the *real* edges, by a *real* fool who *stands* too *close* to *death* and *measures.* *(they study you)* You've a *traveller's* look. *Restless* feet. Tell me — and be *honest*, I can always *tell* — have you ever stood somewhere the world went *thin?* A *causeway*, a *grey* shore, a place where the *air* tasted of *pennies* and *cold?* *(they lean forward, hungry)* Because if you *have*, friend — *(a grin)* — then you and I have a *very* great deal to *talk* about, and I have a *very* great deal to *ask.*

nodes.speaker: Sennet
nodes.text: *(They sit amid the spilling map like a captain on the deck of a ship made of parchment, pen behind one ear, ink to the elbows, alight with the particular joy of a person who has finally met someone who might *understand.*)* So. *(grinning)* Ask the *mad* mapmaker your questions. I'll answer *all* of them — I've *no* secrets, only *coordinates* — and then, if you'll *humour* a dying surveyor, I'll ask *you* a few that no *living* soul has *ever* been able to *answer.* *(a wink)* Fair *trade?* A *question* for a *bearing?*

choices.text: "Why this, Sennet? Of all the maps a cartographer could make — kingdoms, coastlines, coin — why chart the place that's killing you to chart it?"

[INSIGHT] *(if the apprentice told you the cost)* "Your apprentice counts your days, Sennet. Two, maybe three more 'field trips.' You know that. You're not a fool about the world — only about *yourself*, the one country you've never surveyed. You're racing your own death to finish a map *of* death. Doesn't the irony of that ever stop your pen?"

*(offer the one thing a cartographer of the dead has never had)* "Stop standing at the edges, Sennet. You don't need to — not anymore. I've *been* in the deep country. I go *in* and come *out.* So here's my offer: don't lose another year to a spyglass. Come with me. I'll be your *surveyor* where no living foot can go, and you'll finish your atlas without it finishing *you.* The blank middle of your map — I'll walk it, and report."

"There's a buyer circling your wagon, Sennet. Goodman Vael. He doesn't want a comfort-copy — he wants the working survey, the door-map. And a map of every door into death is a dangerous thing to sell. Do you know what you've actually drawn?"

[PERSUASION] "Then let me hire your eyes, Sennet — for a road that needs them. I'm bound for places that aren't on any living map, and I'd rather walk them with a surveyor who's already half-charted the dark than blunder in blind. Throw in with me. Map the road as we walk it."
"Good mapping to you, Sennet. Mind the edges." *(take your leave)*

nodes.speaker: Sennet
nodes.text: *(The grin gentles into something older, and they trace, absently, the grey coastline they were inking.)* …Why. *(quietly)* Everyone asks *why.* *(they look up)* Because, friend, there is a *map* of *every* place a *living* soul might *go* — kingdoms charted to the *inch*, oceans *sounded*, the *whole* bright *world* surveyed and *sold* — and there is *no* map, *none*, of the *one* place *every* soul goes *without fail.* *(their voice roughens)* When my *mother* died — unbought, like me, like *half* of us — the *priests* could not tell me *where* she'd *gone.* Only that she'd gone *somewhere* I should *fear.* And I thought: *that* is *obscene.* That we *chart* the *trade-routes* of *spice* to the *farthing* and leave the *one* journey *every* mother and child and *fool* of us *takes* as a *blank* on the page marked *'here be terrors.'* *(fierce, quiet)* So I am making the *map* I *wish* I'd had. So the *next* soul who loses their *mother* can *look* at a *true chart* and *see* — *not* a terror, *not* a *blank* — but a *country.* A *real* place, with a *grey* shore and a *cold* river and a *far* bank, that their *love* went *to* and not merely *out* of. *(they meet your eyes)* A map doesn't *save* anyone, friend. But it *un-blanks* the *fear.* And an *un-blanked* fear is a *grief* you can *hold* instead of a *horror* you can only *flee.* *(a crooked smile)* *That's* why. It's worth a *year* of warmth a *survey*, to *un-blank* the *worst* page in *everyone's* atlas.

nodes.speaker: Sennet
nodes.text: *(The pen stops. For a long moment the manic brightness drains, and what's left is a tired, clear-eyed soul who knows *exactly* the size of the bet they've placed.)* …No. *(quietly)* It doesn't stop my pen. *(they set it down)* You're *right* — about *all* of it. Tibb's count is *good*; the lad's better at *that* arithmetic than I am, which is *why* I keep him, and *why* I can't *look* at him lately. *(they flex the left hand — the one Tibb told you to watch; it does not fully open anymore, the cold gone into the joints)* Two surveys. *Three*, if I'm *lucky* and *foolish*, which I reliably *am.* *(they meet your eyes)* And *yes* — racing my *death* to finish a map *of* death is *exactly* as *absurd* as it *sounds*, and I have *laughed* about it, and the laugh is *real.* *(quietly, the laugh gone)* But here is the thing your *clever* insight *misses*, friend: I am *dying anyway.* *Everyone* is. The *only* question a *dying* soul *gets* to answer is *what they spend the dying ON.* *(fierce)* And I would *rather* spend mine *un-blanking* the *worst* page in *everyone's* atlas than *hoarding* a few *grey* extra *years* in a *chair*, *un-spent*, *afraid.* *(they almost smile)* You think I don't *survey* the country of *my own* death? I survey it *every* morning. It's *close* now. I can *take its bearing* without a *spyglass.* *(quietly)* I just *refuse* to let it be the *only* map I *finish.* …Is *that* a *fool*, friend? Or is it the *one* surveyor *honest* enough to *chart* the thing he's *standing in?*

nodes.speaker: Sennet
nodes.text: *(They stare at you. The pen falls from behind their ear unnoticed. And then a sound comes out of them that is half a laugh and half a sob, the sound of a soul being handed, in one sentence, the thing they had made their *peace* with never having.)* …A *surveyor* for the *deep* country. *(barely)* A *soul* that goes *in* and comes *out.* That I could *send* where my *body* cannot *follow*, who would *come back* and *tell* me the *bearings*, and I would *draw* them, *warm*, *alive*, at a *table*, instead of *losing* a *year* to a *spyglass* a *half-mile* from a *Wall.* *(they grip both your hands, ink and cold mingling)* Do you understand what you've just *offered* a *dying* cartographer? Not a *job.* Not a *partnership.* You've offered me my *atlas* AND my *life* — the *two* things I was *certain* I'd have to *choose* between. *(fierce, weeping, grinning)* *Tibb!* — no, *don't* call Tibb yet, the lad will *cry*, and then *I'll* cry, and we'll *flood* the *map.* *(they pull themselves together, barely)* …*Yes.* *Yes*, a *thousand* times, *yes.* I'll *come.* I'll map the *road* as we *walk* it, I'll *draw* the *deep* country from *your* reports, and I will *live* to *finish* the *one* map that *matters* — *(they look at you, and the manic joy settles into something steadier and deeper)* — because a *Returned* walked up to my *wagon* and decided a *mad mapmaker's* life was *worth* the *saving.* *(quietly)* I'll *earn* it, friend. Every *bearing.* You'll have the *finest*-charted *campaign* any soul ever *walked.* Where do we *start?* — *no*, don't tell me yet. Let me *fetch* my *good* pens. The *deep*-country pens. I've been *saving* them. I knew — *somehow* I *knew* — I'd *meet* the soul I was *saving* them *for.*

nodes.speaker: Sennet
nodes.text: *(They light up like a struck lantern.)* *Map the road as we walk it!* *(they're already gathering pens)* Oh, friend, you say that as though it's a *small* thing, *hiring* a surveyor for a *road* — but a road that *'isn't on any living map'* is the *only* road I have *ever* wanted to *walk*, and you've just *invited* me onto it like it's *nothing.* *(they sling a satchel of charts over one shoulder)* I'm *in.* *Obviously* I'm in. I'd have *followed* you for the *coastline* of your *cold* alone. *(a grin)* I'll *chart* every step — the *thin* places, the *crossings*, the *deep* country you'll *drag* me *near* and, gods *willing*, *not quite into.* *(they pause, and something honest surfaces under the glee)* …And maybe, walking *with* a soul who *comes back* from the *dark*, I'll *learn* to take a *bearing* without *paying* a *year* for it. *Tibb* would *like* that. *Tibb* would like that *very* much. *(they clap your shoulder, ink-handed)* *Lead* on, road-walker. Let's go put the *blank* parts of the *world* on the *page.*

nodes.speaker: Sennet
nodes.text: *(The brightness sharpens into something more dangerous, and they glance toward the soft-spoken man by the wagon.)* …Vael. *(quietly)* I *wondered.* A *fortnight* of *admiring* and *no* purchase — that's not a *customer*, that's a *patient* man *waiting* for the *price* to *drop.* And the price of a *dying* mapmaker's *work* drops *sharply* the day they *die.* *(they set down the pen)* You think I don't *know* what I've *drawn*, friend? *(a hard, clear look — far less mad than they let on)* I sell *comfort-copies* — the *pretty* ones, the *'your-dead-went-to-a-real-place'* ones — to *widows*, for *coppers*, and I'll do it till my *hand* gives out, because that's the *map's* whole *purpose.* *(quietly)* But the *working* survey — the one with the *true bearings* to the *thin* places, the *crossings*, the *doors* — *that* one I keep *here.* *(they tap their temple, then their heart)* And *that* one is *not* for *sale*, to *Vael* or to *anyone*, because I am not a *fool* about the *world*, only about *myself.* I *know* exactly what a *door-map* is worth to a *clever, grieving, dangerous* man. *(fierce)* So if you've come to *help* him *buy* it — we're *done.* But if you've come because *you*, too, can *smell* the *patience* on him and don't *like* it — *(a grin returns, fierce and glad)* — then, friend, you and I are going to get on *famously*, and the *first* place I'll map *with* you is the *road* that *patient* bastard *takes* when we run him *off.*

nodes.speaker: Sennet
nodes.text: *(✦ You don't just offer a job — you describe the road ahead in the surveyor's own language, the thin places and deep country and uncharted dark, and you do it so vividly that the cartographer of the dead realizes they are not being *hired* but *invited home*, to the one country they've spent a lifetime trying to reach.)* *(They are on their feet, scattering pens, incandescent.)* — *the deep country, walked, not glimpsed!* *(they seize your face in both ink-stained hands)* You — you don't even *know* what you just *did*, do you, you said it in *MY* *language*, you said *'thin places'* and *'crossings'* and *'the dark that isn't on any map'* like you'd *read my own field-notes*, like you'd been *waiting* your *whole* life to *meet a mapmaker who'd understand* exactly *as long* as I've been waiting to *meet a road-walker who'd take me!* *(they're laughing, weeping, already packing)* *TIBB!* *TIBB*, fetch the *good* pens, we're — we're *GOING*, lad, the *real* survey, the *deep* country, with a *guide who comes BACK* — *(they round on you, radiant)* I will chart you the *finest* campaign-map any soul has *ever* unrolled by a *campfire.* I will mark *every* danger, *every* door, *every* thin place where the *cold* gets *in.* And when it's *done* — *(fierce, joyful, alive)* — when the *atlas* is *finished* and the *worst* page in *everyone's* book is *un-blanked* — I will *put your name* on the *title cartouche*, road-walker, in the *deep-country* ink, where it will *outlast* us *both.* *Lead.* Gods, *lead*, before I *burst.*

nodes.speaker: Sennet
nodes.text: *(💀 You reach for the rousing recruitment pitch and, somewhere in the middle of it, confidently mispronounce 'cartographer' as 'cartograffer,' and then — committing — refer to their life's work as 'the death-doodles.' There is a pause. A pen rolls off the map. Tibb, somewhere behind you, makes a small strangled sound.)* *(Sennet blinks.)* …The *death-doodles.* *(flatly)* Thirty years of *survey-grade* afterlife *cartography*, four *fingers* lost to the *exposure*, and you've called it — *(they pick the pen back up with enormous dignity)* — *doodles.* *(a long-suffering sigh)* Friend, I was *going* to follow you to the *literal edge of the world.* I was *halfway* out of my *chair.* And then you said *'cartograffer'* and *something died in me* that the *Wall* itself couldn't *kill.* *(they wave you off, wounded)* Go. *Practice* the *word.* It's got a *silent* nothing in it, it's *not* hard. Come *back* when you can *say* what I *am* without making it sound like I *scribble* in the *margins.* *(under their breath, returning to the map)* …*death-doodles.* Thirty *years.* The *gall.*

nodes.speaker: Sennet
nodes.text: *(They laugh — a real, delighted, slightly manic bark — and wave your insight away with an ink-stained hand.)* *Irony!* *(grinning)* Friend, *irony* is the *house wine* of the *dying*, I *drink* it by the *flagon.* *(they tap the map)* *Of course* it's absurd to race my *death* to map *death.* It's the *funniest* thing I've ever *done*, and I once mapped a *brothel* for a *blind* duke. *(a wink)* But you've gone *digging* for a *tragic* nerve and hit only *good humour*, which — between us — is the *one* defence a *dying* fool has that *actually* holds. *(lighter, deflecting)* Ask me about the *map*, road-walker, not the *mapmaker.* The *map's* the *interesting* one. *I'm* just the *fool* who *holds the pen* — and *fools* with *pens*, friend, are a *copper* a *dozen.* It's the *country* that's *priceless.* *Ask* me about the *country.*

nodes.speaker: Sennet
nodes.text: *(They consider you, head tilted, the surveyor weighing a doubtful bearing.)* …A *road* that's not on any *living* map. *(they tap the pen against their teeth)* It's a *pretty* pitch, friend, and you've a *traveller's* feet, I'll grant — but I've been *burned* by *'come walk the interesting road with me'* before. *(a crooked smile)* The *interesting* roads have a *way* of being *interesting* in the *'and then everyone died'* sense, and I've *precisely* enough *life* left to be *choosy* about *which* edge finally *keeps* me. *(they study you)* Show me you're more than a *pretty* pitch — that you've *actually* *been* where you say, that you *come back* — and I'll *follow* you off the *edge* of the *known world* with *bells* on. But I'll not *spend* my *last* surveys on a *stranger's say-so.* *Prove* the *cold* on you is the *real* article, road-walker. *Then* we'll talk *bearings.*

nodes.speaker: Sennet
nodes.text: *(They give you a cheerful, ink-stained salute and bend back to the impossible coastline.)* Mind the edges *yourself*, road-walker. *(a grin)* You've the look of a soul that *stands* too *close* to them — *takes* one to *know* one. *(they dip the pen)* …Come *back*, though, eh? If your *restless* feet take you somewhere the world goes *thin.* I'll want the *bearings.* I *always* want the *bearings.* *(quietly, almost to the map)* And bring them *quick.* I've not got *many* surveys left in this old *hand*, and I'd *hate* to leave the *best* page *blank* for want of a *road-walker* who *came back* a *day* too *late.* *(a wink)* …No pressure. Only the *one* unfinished *atlas* in all the *world.* And the *fool* who'll die holding its *pen.* Off you *go.*
